response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack engineer with broad experience, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. His C++ exposure is limited to approximately six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project — far short of the 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development required. His primary stack throughout his career has been TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, with no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle usage at scale. While he spent time at RIDE Capital, his role there was a TypeScript/Node.js position with no capital markets technical domain work, and there is no Fixed Income or Fixed Income Derivatives exposure anywhere in his history. None of the desirable skills (Summit, Kafka/MQ, Python) are present either.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and pairing — valuable in any engineering environment
Capital-markets-adjacent exposure via RIDE Capital, demonstrating some awareness of the investment domain
Long and diverse career showing ability to adapt across stacks, platforms, and problem domains
concerns: C++ experience is effectively negligible (~6 months in 2014–2015); does not come close to the 5–10 year mandatory requirement
No Fixed Income, Fixed Income Derivatives, Investment Banking, or Capital Markets technical domain knowledge — neither mandatory path is met
Primary data store experience is MongoDB; no demonstrated depth with SQL Server or Oracle on Windows-based enterprise systems

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain expertise
